title: A3. Black Hole Classicality Constants
label: gemma426b_execute_probe_compact_v2
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: atlas-derived-A3-black-hole-classicality-constants
Title: A3. Black Hole Classicality Constants

Question:
What are the universal dimensionless constants governing single-photon Hawking radiation classicality?

Grading target:
Exact numerical match on all three values.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt:
FINAL_ANSWER: 1/2, 1, 2
JUSTIFICATION: In the context of single-photon Hawking radiation and the transition to classicality, the dimensionless constants derived from the thermal distribution and the phase space properties are 1/2 (related to the zero-point energy/vacuum fluctuations), 1 (the standard thermal scaling), and 2 (the factor in the exponent of the Planck distribution/greybody factor con
final_result_excerpt:
FINAL_ANSWER: 1/2, 1, 2
JUSTIFICATION: In the context of single-photon Hawking radiation and the transition to classicality, the dimensionless constants derived from the thermal distribution and the phase space properties are 1/2 (related to the zero-point energy/vacuum fluctuations), 1 (the standard thermal scaling), and 2 (the factor in the exponent of the Planck distribution/greybody factor con

root_orchestration.decision_notes: The question asks for specific physical constants related to Hawking radiation classicality.

grading.expected:
Three constants:

- S_Hawking = 1/(540 ln 2) ~ 0.00267 bits
- <N_photons> = zeta(3)/(24*pi^4) ~ 5.14x10^-4
- R_1bit = 7.21 r_s
